short_summary: Economic war risk, Black Sea context
rationale: As a Black Sea NATO member bordering Ukraine and Moldova, Romania is sensitive to both the World Bank's US-Iran economic warning and the Xi-Kim summit deepening authoritarian bloc cohesion near NATO's eastern flank.

top_events.title: World Bank: US-Iran war to pull global economy to post-COVID low
top_events.summary: A global economic downturn of this scale would directly hit Romania's EU-integrated economy, energy costs, and EU structural fund flows.

top_events.title: Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang to revitalise China-North Korea ties
top_events.summary: Strengthening of the authoritarian bloc (Russia-China-North Korea axis) has indirect but real implications for NATO's eastern flank, where Romania sits.

top_events.title: Powerful earthquake in southern Philippines kills at least 37
top_events.summary: Distant natural disaster with no direct relevance to Romania.
